004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.client;
018
019/**
020 * Lifecycle interceptor for REST calls made by {@link RestClient}.
021 *
022 * <p>
023 * Registered on the client builder via {@link RestClient.Builder#interceptors(RestCallInterceptor...)}.
024 * All callbacks have default no-op implementations so implementations only need to override the hooks they care about.
025 *
026 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
027 * <p class='bjava'>
028 *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>builder</jsm>()
029 *       .transport(<jv>transport</jv>)
030 *       .interceptors(new RestCallInterceptor() {
031 *          &#64;Override
032 *          <jk>public void</jk> onConnect(RestRequest req, RestResponse res) {
033 *             System.<jf>out</jf>.println(<js>"Response: "</js> + res.getStatusCode());
034 *          }
035 *       })
036 *       .build();
037 * </p>
038 *
039 * <p>
040 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
041 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
042 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
043 * (and possibly earlier).
044 *
045 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
046 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
047 * </ul>
048 *
049 * @since 9.2.1
050 */
051@SuppressWarnings({
052   "java:S112" // Interceptor SPI callbacks intentionally declare 'throws Exception' so implementations may abort a call with any exception type.
053})
054public interface RestCallInterceptor {
055
056   /**
057    * Called before the transport request is built and sent.
058    *
059    * <p>
060    * Use this hook to add headers, modify query parameters, or short-circuit the call.
061    *
062    * @param req The request being built. Never <jk>null</jk>.
063    * @throws Exception If the call should be aborted.
064    */
065   default void onInit(RestRequest req) throws Exception {}
066
067   /**
068    * Called after a response has been received from the transport.
069    *
070    * <p>
071    * Use this hook to inspect status codes, validate headers, or modify how the response is interpreted.
072    *
073    * @param req The request that was sent. Never <jk>null</jk>.
074    * @param res The response received. Never <jk>null</jk>.
075    * @throws Exception If a post-connect error should be raised.
076    */
077   default void onConnect(RestRequest req, RestResponse res) throws Exception {}
078
079   /**
080    * Called when the request is being closed (in the {@code finally} block of {@link RestRequest#run()}).
081    *
082    * <p>
083    * This is called regardless of whether the call succeeded or failed, and is always invoked before
084    * the logger receives its entry. Use it for cleanup, metrics recording, or span/trace finalization.
085    *
086    * @param req The request. Never <jk>null</jk>.
087    * @param res The response, or <jk>null</jk> if the transport failed before a response was received.
088    * @throws Exception If an error occurs during close handling.
089    */
090   default void onClose(RestRequest req, RestResponse res) throws Exception {}
091}
